description: This bird was a jaeger, identified by its mostly chocolate brown plumage with barred undertail coverts, bill thinner than a gull's bill, and white primary tips which were more pointed than that shown on any of the gull species.   This is was what I believe is a dark intermediate morph.   Bill was strongly bicolored, gray with black tip to distall 1/4 of bill.   Head was blocky with NO contrasting hindnecked coloration.  Mantle was barred with rusty edges Base of tail (dorsal surface) was barred out half way to its tip Undertail was heavily barred and this feature was the most distinctive feature at rest and while in flight Pot-bellied look, looked like it was not centered properly to be able to fly, big-ass chest, wide too Flew with powerful but SLOW wingbeats, often banking and landing in the H20  Wingtips with outer three (at least) primaries very contrasty whitish with remainder of dark primaries. Underwing very mottled and quite noticeable in flight.  Arm of bird very wide, compared to rapidly tapering hand with ponted tips.  Tail short. Only on occasion did small rounded tail projections become just barely visibleBehaviors: Fed alone, did not chase any gulls or other birds. Appeared attracted to boat wakes, often flying a short ways and landing in the wake, sometimes picking "stuff" off of the water surface.  Strong flier.   Seemed to prefer resting in water in area of south bank where point sticks into reservoir.Call: none givenPlumage: juvenile
